title: Германия запретила мужчинам моложе 45 лет покидать страну без разрешения
description: В Германии все мужчины в возрасте от 17 до 45 лет теперь должны получать разрешение Бундесвера, если они хотят покинуть страну более чем на три месяца. Как пишет Berliner Zeitung, поправка к закону о модернизации военной службы действует с 1 января 2026 года, при этом изменения остались незамеченными.

title: Trump seeks massive $1.5 trillion defence budget amid Iran war

description: It’s been a week that began with President Trump addressing the nation for the first time since the Iran War began five weeks ago. Expectations that he might signal an end to the conflict—or announce a ground offensive or a split from NATO—were unmet. Instead, his 18-minute speech echoed his social media rhetoric: a mix of “we’re almost done” and threats of escalating attacks, including striking every Iranian power plant if a deal isn’t reached.

title: 8 killed, 95 wounded in US-Israeli attacks on Iran bridge
description: At least eight civilians were killed and 95 others wounded in US and Israeli attacks on a bridge in Iran's northern Alborz province on Thursday, the official news agency IRNA reported. According to the report, the B1 bridge, located in the provincial capital Karaj, is one of the highest bridges in the Middle East and among the most complicated projects in Iran. It was targeted twice with missiles on Thursday.

title: Viktor Orbán courts voters in neighboring Romania as Hungary’s election tightens

In a bid to cling onto power, Orban is looking beyond the Hungarian borders and specifically targeting voters in neighboring Romania.

Spain implemented a 30-cent reduction at the pump, Italy has capped some taxes, and Lithuania has cut train ticket prices in half.

description: Karim Al-Haj Hussein survived an Israeli strike on his home in Lebanon’s Baalbek that killed his family members.

title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump as Fighting Rages and Missiles Fly
description: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.

title: Man files war crimes complaint in France over deadly 2024 IDF strike in Beirut
description:
French-Lebanese artist Ali Cherri says his parents were killed, wants probe into 'why civilians were targeted'; IDF yet to comment, has said it follows international law

title: Trump sacks attorney general Bondi, Hegseth asks US Army Chief of Staff to step down
description: US President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday after months of strained relations and named his former personal lawyer to serve as the acting chief of the Justice Department. Pentagon ​chief ​Pete Hegseth has asked the ​U.S. ‌Army ⁠Chief ‌of Staff ⁠Randy George to step ​down ‌and take immediate ‌retirement. The Guardian Washington DC bureau chief David Smith reports.

title: Oil shock triggers global price spikes as Iran war drags on
description: Fuel shocks from the US-Israel war on Iran are rippling worldwide, as Strait of Hormuz disruptions push prices higher.

title: Iran says army forces are ready to repel US-Israeli attacks
description: Iran says it is ready to counter any US-Israeli attacks, insisting its military capabilities remain intact.

title: Russia sending second ship with oil to Cuba amid US blockade
description: Announcement comes days after Russian tanker brought some 700,000 barrels of crude to Cuba in response to energy crisis.

title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

title: The Race for the Future of the Moon
description: The Artemis II mission isn’t just about science and exploration. It’s part of a high-stakes space race between the U.S. and China.

description: The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. title: Hegseth removes Army's top general during Iran war
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has asked Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George to leave his post, a Defense official confirmed to Axios. Why it matters: George is the Army's most senior general and is being removed during the Iran war.
